---
title: "Dookie2024 Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---
# Libraries
```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(targets)
library(tidyverse)
library(visNetwork)
```

```{r}
#setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
setwd(this.path::this.dir())
```

# Check pipeline status
```{r}
tar_glimpse()
```

```{r}
tar_visnetwork()
```

```{r}
tar_visnetwork(targets_only = TRUE)
```

# Construct pipeline
```{r}
# targets::tar_make(callr_function = NULL) # for debugging
targets::tar_make()
```

# Destroy pipeline (off by default)
```{r, warning=FALSE, eval = FALSE }
# tar_destroy()
# targets::tar_destroy(ask = FALSE)
```


```{r}
tar_load(check_depend)
tar_load(df_all_obs_files)
tar_load(file_pheno_haun_obs)
tar_load(df_haun_pheno_dates)
tar_load(df_pheno_interp)
tar_load(df_simNameByCult)
tar_load(df_obs_meta_data)
tar_load(list_observed_dfs_raw_clean)
tar_load(df_list_PCDS)
tar_load(df_PCDS_int)
tar_load(df_dateStageTargetReached)
tar_load(df_haun_pheno_dates)
df1<-tar_read(list_observed_dfs_raw_clean)
tar_load(df_apsimStageInput_haunBased)
tar_invalidate(df_stages_Observ)
```

```{r}
tar_invalidate(config)
tar_make(config)
tar_read(config)$varName_addedToObserv
df2<- tar_read(df_apsimStageInput_haunBased)
```

```{r}
# 1. Get the master list of all expected simulations
master_sims <- tar_read(df_simNameByCult)$SimulationName

# 2. Check the final output
final_sims <- tar_read(df_apsimStageInput_haunBased)$SimulationName
print(paste("Final output has", length(final_sims), "simulations. Expected:", length(master_sims)))

# 3. Identify exactly which ones are missing
missing_sims <- setdiff(master_sims, final_sims)
print("Missing Simulations:")
print(missing_sims)

# 4. TRACE BACKWARDS: Find out where they dropped!
# Did they make it through the Haun update?
print(length(tar_read(df_apsimStageInput)$SimulationName))

# Did they make it through the PCDS target finder?
print(length(tar_read(df_dateStageTargetReached)$SimulationName))

# Did they even exist in the interpolated PCDS data?
print(length(unique(tar_read(df_PCDS_int)$SimulationName)))
```

```{r}
require(dplyr)
require(targets)

# Pull the raw data right after it was read from Excel
raw_data <- tar_read(list_observed_dfs_raw)
raw_bound <- dplyr::bind_rows(raw_data$data)
all_raw_sims <- unique(raw_bound$SimulationName)

# Search for ANY simulation name containing "Gregory" (ignoring capitals)
gregory_search <- grep("Gregory", all_raw_sims, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)

message("Found these Gregory-like names in the raw data:")
print(gregory_search)

# Let's also check Wedgetail just in case
wedge_search <- grep("Wedge", all_raw_sims, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
message("Found these Wedgetail-like names in the raw data:")
print(wedge_search)
```

```{r}
require(dplyr)
require(targets)

target_sim <- "DookieEVA2024SowApril_BigRed"
pcds_list <- tar_read(df_list_PCDS)

# Flatten the list into one master dataframe, tagging the source name
gregory_timeline <- dplyr::bind_rows(pcds_list, .id = "df_name") %>%
  dplyr::filter(SimulationName == target_sim) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Date)

# Print the full timeline
print(gregory_timeline, n = Inf)
```
```{r}
require(dplyr)
require(targets)

# 1. Pull the rawest data right after it was read from Excel
raw_nested <- tar_read(list_observed_dfs_raw)

# 2. Flatten it so we can search the whole thing
raw_flat <- dplyr::bind_rows(raw_nested$data, .id = "df_name")

# 3. Search for anything remotely resembling "Big" or "Red"
bigred_search <- raw_flat %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    grepl("Big", SimulationName, ignore.case = TRUE) | 
    grepl("Red", SimulationName, ignore.case = TRUE) |
    grepl("Big", df_name, ignore.case = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  # Just show us where it is and what it's named
  dplyr::select(df_name, dplyr::any_of(c("SimulationName", "Date", "Cultivar"))) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

print(bigred_search, n = Inf)


```
